When a player is "safe at first," it means that the batter successfully reached first base without being tagged out. This can happen in several ways: 1. By hitting the ball and reaching first base before the defense can tag them out. 2. By drawing a walk (four balls), which allows the batter to go to first base. 3. Therefore, the sentence "Max Scherzer was safe at first" is plausible if it refers to a situation where Scherzer, as a batter, reached first base safely.
